subHeader: Africapolis is a research and data visualisation tool used to map, analyse and understand urbanisation and urban growth in Africa

list.title: A new approach to defining urban.
list.subTitle: Defining urban
list.description: Starting from the ground up Africapolis adopts a systematic and consistent definition of urban. By combining demographic sources, satellite and aerial imagery, Africapolis enables comparative and long-term analyses of urban dynamics. It covers all 13,000 urban agglomerations with more than 10,000 inhabitants in 54 African countries.

list.title: Mapping a new urban reality.
list.subTitle: Urbanisation dynamics and drivers
list.description: In 2015, more than 50% of Africans lived in urban agglomerations. Cities, urban planning and management are key to delivering on the SDGs and a New Urban Agenda. Africapolis data and evidence supports cities and governments to make urban areas more inclusive, productive and sustainable.

list.title: Understanding what drives urbanisation in Africa today.
list.subtitle: Beyond large cities
list.description: Africa has the fastest urban growth in the world. By 2050, Africa's urban areas will be home to an additional 950 million people. Capturing how this growth is changing the size, structure and functional features of urban networks is key for managing and planning future trends.

list.title: Mapping a new urban reality.
list.subTitle: Informing policies for Africa's urban future
list.description: In 2015, more than 50% of Africans lived in urban agglomerations. Cities, urban planning and management are key to delivering on the SDGs and a New Urban Agenda. Africapolis data and evidence supports cities and governments to make urban areas more inclusive, productive and sustainable.

list.title: A new approach to defining urban.
list.subTitle: Defining urban
list.description: Starting from the ground up Africapolis adopts a systematic and consistent definition of urban. By combining demographic sources, satellite and aerial imagery, Africapolis enables comparative and long-term analyses of urban dynamics. It covers all 13,000 urban agglomerations with more than 10,000 inhabitants in 54 African countries.

list.title: Understanding what drives urbanisation in Africa today.
list.subTitle: Urbanisation dynamics and drivers
list.description: Africa has the fastest urban growth in the world. By 2050, Africa's urban areas will be home to an additional 950 million people. Capturing how this growth is changing the size, structure and functional features of urban networks is key for managing and planning future trends.

list.title: There are only few big cities but many small ones.
list.subTitle: Beyond large cities
list.description: In Africa, as elsewhere, there are only few big cities but many small ones. The continent has 11 urban areas with more than five million inhabitants and more than 7,500 secondary cities with fewer than 500,000 inhabitants. Yet, many of these urban agglomerations are absent from most international databases.

list.title: Urban agglomeration network
list.subTitle: Distance is a physical as well as a socio-economic concept.
list.description: A total of 320,162 pairings are made by connecting all urban agglomerations that are 120 kilometres or less from one another.

list.title: Cross-border agglomerations in Africa
list.subTitle: In Africa, cities in close proximity are often located across the border from one another.
list.description: More than 42 million people (almost 8% of total urban population) of the continent live in these 645 cross-border cities.

list.title: Top 50 largest agglomerations in Africa
list.subTitle: Twenty of the 50 largest agglomeration by built-up area are in Nigeria and Kenya.
list.description: One of the main drivers of urban growth in Africa is the expansion of built-up areas.

list.title: Explore
list.subTitle: Distance is a physical as well as a socio-economic concept.
list.description: Explore data for more than 13,000 urban agglomerations in 54 African countries.

list.title: Country reports
list.subTitle: In Africa, cities in close proximity are often located across the border from one another.
list.description: A look into the urbanisation dynamics of 54 countries since 1950 and how they are shaping new urban realities.

list.title: Data
list.subTitle: In Africa, cities in close proximity are often located across the border from one another.
list.description: Build custom data visualisations on urbanisation in Africa and download the data.
